title: infer_damaged_sign data payload
description: For each `X` (damaged-position token) in an eBL transliteration, suggest the most-probable sign based on bigram context across the eBL corpus. Scoring: geometric mean of P(sign | prev_sign) and P(sign | next_sign), with optional period/genre conditioning from the v0.13.1 metadata enrichment dataset. Bigram index built lazily from all-signs-full.json (36,498 tablets, ~1.8M bigrams). Wrapped in the StructuredEnvelope from envelope.schema.json.

tablet_id.description: Museum number when the caller passed a tablet_id; null when raw signs were passed inline.

input_signs_length.description: Number of whitespace-separated tokens in the input signs string.

damaged_positions.description: Indices of all X/x tokens in the input. If `position` was passed in the call, only that single position is inferred (but this array still lists all damage positions in the input).

snippet.description: 5-7 token window around the damaged position with [?] marking the inference target.

score.description: Combined probability score (higher = more likely). Geometric mean of P(sign|prev) and P(sign|next) with optional period/genre boosts.

total_corpus_count.description: Unconditional corpus frequency of the candidate sign.

period.description: Period the caller requested (Neo_Assyrian, Neo_Babylonian, Old_Babylonian, etc.). Present only when caller passed it.

genre.description: Genre the caller requested (literary, divinatory, magical_ritual, administrative, etc.). Present only when caller passed it.

applied.description: True if conditioning was actually applied (only works when v0.13.1 tablet metadata covers the requested period/genre).

warnings.description: Optional warnings about out-of-range positions, missing conditioning metadata, etc.
